verbatim_text: My work was the first to highlight this connection and many works in this area have built upon this insight. Second, it's actually a practical way to design training objectives to learn algorithms. That is the objective, which is if you optimize on your training set, you can learn good algorithms. So how does it do that? Let's first look at the test objective. This is where you sample a problem from a population of test problems. And for each such problem, you sample a bunch of cases from the algorithm. You look at the terminal rewards. This is the test objective. We want an algorithm that at the end of the day is giving us correct responses. So you want it to maximize this terminal rewards at the end of the day. But during training, we don't have access to this population of test problems. We do have a training data set. So we do the typical thing in machine learning.
